_meta.source: Extracted from starfi5h/AlchemyFactoryCalculator develop branch engine code (DB v41, gameVersion 0.5.0.4471, 2026-06-03) and the official v0.5.4467 patch notes (Steam announcement gid 1833968530899984, 2026-06-02)

cauldron.description: Deterministic 3-input combiner. As of v0.5.4467 (June 2, 2026) the additional output-generation multiplier was REMOVED by the developer; output selection is now purely nearest-target. Items have cauldronCost (input value), cauldronTarget (output selection target), and cauldronMulti (distance weighting).

model.inputs: exactly 3 items, each contributing its cauldronCost
model.outputSelection: output = argmin over items-with-cauldronTarget of |T - cauldronTarget| * cauldronMulti; ties broken by lower item id

craftTimeAndHeat.description: Piecewise-linear interpolation over the OUTPUT item's cauldronTarget

cauldron.machineNote: Cauldron and Advanced Cauldron carry machines.heatCost = -1 in the DB: their heat draw is recipe/output-dependent (see craftTimeAndHeat), not a fixed rate.

advancedAthanorCatalysts.description: Added in v0.5.4467 (June 2026). The Advanced Athanor runs standard Athanor recipes at the same heat consumption as a standard Athanor. Recipes carry a ChargeCost; inserting a catalyst consumes ChargeCost / catalystCharges catalyst items per batch. Catalysts modify the recipe as follows.

Unstable Catalyst.effect: replaces recipe outputs with the recipe's unstableOutputs (altered product probabilities/mix)

Resonant Catalyst.effect: replaces recipe outputs with resonantOutputs (generates all products simultaneously)

Eternal Catalyst.effect: recipe consumes no material inputs (only catalyst charges)

advancedAthanorCatalysts.stackingNote: Catalysts can combine (the calculator applies eternal -> unstable -> resonant -> fertile in that order; fertile doubles whatever output set is active).

heatAndFuel.description: Heat-using machines attach to a heat source (machines.parent, e.g. Stone Furnace) and occupy slotsRequired of the parent's slots. Fuel items carry items.heat (energy). Burned heat is multiplied by fuelMult from the Fuel Efficiency skill.
heatAndFuel.machineHeatDraw: heatPerSec = machine.heatCost * speedMult (+ share of parent.heatSelf split across occupied slots)
heatAndFuel.heatPerBatch: heatCost-per-item = heatPerSec * (recipe.baseTime / speedMult) / batchYield  — speedMult cancels for the machine's own draw, so faster factories do not save heat per item
heatAndFuel.selfFuelLoops: Loops like Charcoal-fired Charcoal production are solvable: net fuel output = gross heat produced - heat consumed by the production chain. The starfi5h calculator exposes gold-per-power (G/P) ratios for choosing the cheapest heat source.

fertilizer.description: Crops grown in Nursery/Seed Plot/World Tree Nursery consume nutrients (recipe.nutrientCost or item.nutrientCost). Fertilizer items carry items.nutrientValue, multiplied by fertMult from the Fertilizer Efficiency skill. Crop plots have maxFertility on items. Nursery crop recipes have no baseTime; growth is nutrient-driven.
fertilizer.selfFertLoops: Self-sustaining fertilizer loops (crops -> fertilizer -> crops) are solvable the same way as self-fuel; the calculator exposes gold-per-nutrient-value (G/V) ratios.

logistics.beltSpeed: 60 items/min base; see skills.json logistics_efficiency for the leveling formula
logistics.conveyorRefactor: v0.5.4467 'Refactoring Conveyor System' — concrete throughput changes not documented in patch notes; treat current belt numbers as needing in-game verification
logistics.railSystem: Rail Logistics System (8x transport capacity, gravity acceleration) is on the official roadmap for Summer 2026 but NOT shipped as of 2026-06-12

economy.prices: items carry buyPrice (Purchasing Portal), sellPrice (46 sellable items), wholesalePrice on some items; coins are items (Copper/Silver/Gold Coin + x50 variants) crafted at Bank Portal
economy.junePatchNote: Black Powder sellPrice AND heat were both doubled in v0.5.4467 — any pre-June economic data for it is wrong

throughputModel.machineOutputRate: outputsPerMin = (60 / (recipe.baseTime / speedMult)) * batchYield, where batchYield = recipe output count * alchemyMult (yield-multiplier machines only; Thermal Extractor x3 extra)
throughputModel.machinesNeeded: targetRate / machineOutputRate, fractional machines rounded up per line segment
